summary: No secrets leak in logs. PAYLOAD_API_KEY resolved at call time only, never passed to console.*. Authorization header not present in any log statement. 409 dedup handled silently. sanitizeError() correctly masks BOT_TOKEN in Telegram API URLs.

findings.finding: Comment on line 49 mentions 'PAYLOAD_API_KEY' in plain text — this is a comment, not a log, so no security risk. Just confirming awareness.
findings.reason: Comments in source code do not constitute secret leakage. The variable name itself is not a secret.

findings.finding: Error log on 500 correctly outputs only res.status and a hardcoded URL string — no headers, no key. Compliant with security requirement.

BOT_TOKEN read from legacy .env file via regex — token value stored only in BOT_TOKEN const, used only in TG_API/TG_FILE URL templates. sanitizeError() masks the token in error messages. Pattern is sound.
